description: Imperfective 'si' catches an event in progress rather than viewing it as a whole. Habit belongs to 'ro' (HAB), which describes recurrence across occasions; 'si' stays with the event now underway.
articulatory_notes: Air passes without voicing along the tongue's central groove for 's' and opens into high-front 'i'. Stress fills the sole syllable.
sound_symbolism: The sustained sibilant can continue as long as the breath does. It offers a physical mnemonic for an event viewed in progress.
usage_notes: Tense precedes 'si', and voice follows it. 'To si' gives a past progressive event and 'so si' a future progressive event. Because a clause admits one aspect particle, 'si' never combines with habitual 'ro'.
